concepts.title: Polymers are high molecular weight compounds
concepts.description: Polymers are large molecules made up of repeating units called monomers. They are often found in natural and synthetic materials.

concepts.title: Types of Polymers
concepts.description: Polymers can be broadly classified into two types: Natural polymers (e.g., cellulose, proteins, DNA, and RNA) and Synthetic polymers (e.g., nylon, polyester, polythene, and PVC).

concepts.title: Properties of Polymers
concepts.description: Polymers exhibit unique properties such as high molecular weight, elasticity, strength, and resistance to chemicals and heat.

concepts.title: Confusing Natural and Synthetic Polymers
concepts.description: Students often confuse natural and synthetic polymers. To avoid this, remember that natural polymers are biodegradable and occur naturally, while synthetic polymers are man-made and may take hundreds of years to decompose.

concepts.title: Polymerization Reactions
concepts.description: Polymers are formed through polymerization reactions, which can be addition or condensation reactions. Addition reactions involve the addition of monomers to form a polymer, while condensation reactions involve the removal of a small molecule (such as water) to form a polymer.

concepts.title: Tacticity in Polymers
concepts.description: Tacticity refers to the arrangement of side groups in a polymer chain. There are three types of tacticity: isotactic, syndiotactic, and atactic.

concepts.title: Applications of Polymers
concepts.description: Polymers have numerous applications in our daily lives, including textiles, packaging materials, adhesives, coatings, and medical devices.
